blurb: FaceTime audio and video calls are protected with end-to-end encryption using SRTP with AES-256 for media streams. DTLS provides key negotiation and authentication at call setup, with keys established between endpoint devices via Apple's STUN/TURN relay infrastructure. Apple's relay servers facilitate NAT traversal but never receive or process media content – they relay only ciphertext. For Group FaceTime, each participant generates individual session keys; a per-conference key ladder distributes media decryption keys to all authorized participants. FaceTime identities are validated through IDS, binding calls to specific Apple Accounts and their registered device public keys.
